title: fvs-modern: Modernized Forest Vegetation Simulator with Bayesian Calibration

creators.name: Weiskittel, Aaron R.
creators.affiliation: University of Maine, Center for Research on Sustainable Forests

description:
A modernized fork of the USDA Forest Vegetation Simulator (FVS): Fortran 77 to Fortran 90 source conversion across all 25 geographic variants, turnkey deployment (Docker, platform installers, Python and REST wrappers), and Bayesian hierarchical calibration of the growth, mortality, height-diameter, crown, and recruitment components against national FIA remeasurement data, with posterior-predictive uncertainty.

This version (2026.06.1) adds the disturbance-aware FIA benchmark (the FVS over-prediction is shown to be largely a benchmark-design artifact of unsimulated harvest, not a growth-equation bias), an out-of-sample-validated keyword adjustment layer (brms site-specific maximum SDI, density-dependent recruitment, per-species basal area increment), the single-framework four-arm comparison of default, keyword-calibrated, and fvs-conus species-free equations, a CONUS-consistent crown-width (MCW) consensus recovered from the variant CCF coefficients, and the fvs-conus component models extended so every component depends on both forest type and ecoregion in species-dependent and species-free forms. Released after a full all-variant build and regression test.
notes: Dual-licensed: MIT for original Python, R, and deployment code; CC0-1.0 for the USDA FVS Fortran source inherited from upstream (see LICENSE). New version of concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19802673.
